error_signature: blkloopinit unsupported delayed assignment to array inside for loops non-delayed is ok
raw_error_example:
%Error-BLKLOOPINIT: <FILE>:<N>: Unsupported: Delayed assignment to array inside for loops (non-delayed is ok - see docs)
  s[i] <= '0;
root_cause: Verilator does not support non-blocking (delayed) assignment to an unpacked array element inside a `for` loop within an `always_ff` block. The RTL typically resets an accumulator/state array like `for (i=0; i<TAPS; i++) s[i] <= '0;` which Icarus Verilog accepts but Verilator rejects. Blocking assignment (`=`) inside the loop is fine; non-blocking (`<=`) is not.
fix_rule: Replace the for-loop of non-blocking array writes with ONE of: (1) a `generate` block that produces a separate `always_ff` per array element, (2) a manually unrolled sequence of `s[0] <= '0; s[1] <= '0; ...`, or (3) a packed array that can be assigned as a whole (`s <= '0;`). Do NOT try to mix blocking and non-blocking — keep the reset style consistent with the rest of the always_ff block.
fix_snippet:
// WRONG (Verilator rejects):
always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
  if (!rst_n) begin
    for (int i = 0; i < TAPS; i++) s[i] <= '0;
  end else begin
    ...
  end
 end

// FIX A — generate block (preferred, scales cleanly):
generate
  for (genvar g = 0; g < TAPS; g++) begin : reset_s
    always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
      if (!rst_n) s[g] <= '0;
      else        s[g] <= next_s[g];
    end
  end
endgenerate

// FIX B — packed array reset as a whole:
logic signed [TAPS-1:0][ACC_WIDTH-1:0] s;
always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
  if (!rst_n) s <= '0;   // one-shot reset, legal in Verilator
  else begin
    for (int i = 0; i < TAPS; i++) s[i] <= next_s[i];  // still illegal — see FIX A
  end
end
